docket_entries.description: MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DECLARATION IN SUPPORT THEREOF (TRANSACTION ID # 200002630) FILED BY PETITIONER CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE HEARING SET FOR JAN-06-2020 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE MAKES NO SENSE, AND IS DENIED. RESPONDENT DORA MARTINEZ DID NOT FILE THIS ACTION; PETITIONER CSAA DID, BY FILING AN "INITIATING PETITION IN UNINSURED MOTORIST ARBITRRATION [SIC]" ON OCTOBER 28, 2019. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT CSAA EVER PROPERLY SERVED THE PETITION ON RESPONDENT. ACCORDING TO THE PETITION, ON MAY 13, 2014, RESPONDENT DEMANDED ARBITRATION UNDER THE UNINSURED MOTORIST PROVISION OF HER AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE POLICY ISSUED BY CSAA, AND CSAA AGREED TO ARBITRATION. IF, AS CSAA CONTENDS, RESPONDENT HAS FAILED TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY, CSAA'S REMEDY IS TO SEEK DISMISSAL OF THE ARBITRATION. IF, ON THE OTHER HAND, CSAA WISHES VOLUNTARILY TO DISMISS ITS OWN ACTION, THERE'S NOTHING TO STOP IT FROM DOING SO. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: CHRISTINE JORDAN CSR#12262, EMAIL: CJORDAN@SFTC.ORG
PETITION FILED BY PETITIONER CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE AS TO RESPONDENT MARTINEZ, DORA
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-06-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Prosecute; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Daniel J. Jungwirth, Esq. of CARBONE SMITH & KOYAMA, T:408.983.1675, for petitioner/moving party, on Court Call;
No other appearances.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PETITIONER CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Prosecute, as follows:

Petitioner CSAA Insurance Exchange's motion to dismiss action for failure to prosecute makes no sense, and is denied. Respondent Dora Martinez did not file this action; Petitioner CSAA did, by filing an "Initiating Petition in Uninsured Motorist Arbitrration [sic]" on October 28, 2019. It does not appear that CSAA ever properly served the petition on Respondent. According to the Petition, on May 13, 2014, Respondent demanded arbitration under the uninsured motorist provision of her automobile insurance policy issued by CSAA, and CSAA agreed to arbitration. If, as CSAA contends, Respondent has failed to respond to discovery, CSAA's remedy is to seek dismissal of the arbitration. If, on the other hand, CSAA wishes voluntarily to dismiss its own action, there's nothing to stop it from doing so.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org
